summary: Solid show with bright Bertha and psychedelic China Cat moments
blurb: The set swings from a shaky start to strong highlights like Bertha, Black Peter and an inspired China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider pairing. Jerry’s guitar shines while his vocals wobble on a few numbers.
review: October 11 1989 at Meadowlands Arena delivered a mixed‑bag performance that still offers plenty for Deadheads to dig into. The first set limps out of the gate with a tentative "Let The Good Times Roll" and vocal missteps on "Greatest Story Ever Told," but recovers as "Bertha" erupts and "When I Paint My Masterpiece" showcases Jerry’s emotive soloing. The second set is where the night truly clicks: a crisp matrix mix captures Brent’s synth‑driven psychedelia, propelling the "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" segue into a soaring jam, while "Althea," "Cassidy" and the deep groove of "Black Peter" keep the energy high. Even with vocal slips on "Terrapin Station" and some uneven moments, Phil’s booming bass and Bob’s in‑your‑face guitar keep the band locked in. The encore “It’s All Over Now Baby Blue” rounds out a night that, despite its flaws, offers several standout moments for fans of the late‑80s era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four out of five recordings describe the show positively, highlighting strong performances (Althea, Cassidy, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Black Peter) and excellent matrix sound. Only one recording is mixed, noting vocal slips and a few weak songs, so overall sentiment is moderately positive, warranting a +1.4 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard with clear mix and solid capture of the show’s high points, especially "Bertha" and "When I Paint My Masterpiece."

key_highlights: Bertha erupts early, giving the set a lift after a tentative start.
"China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" shines in a superb matrix mix, showcasing Brent’s synth‑driven psychedelia.
Black Peter and Althea deliver intense, emotionally charged jams that stand out among late‑80s performances.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers strong, emotive solos on "When I Paint My Masterpiece" and "Black Peter," but his vocals slip on "Loser" and "Terrapin Station."
band_performance.Phil: Provides big, bouncy bass that drives the band forward; harmonies are prominent especially on built‑to‑last material.
band_performance.Bob: Guitar work is in‑your‑face throughout, with a calmer Dylan‑style vocal approach on some numbers but occasional monotony noted on "Estimated Prophet."
band_performance.Brent: Not at his best on "Just A Little Light," yet brings aggressive lyrical presence on "Bertha" and powers the psychedelic texture via MIDI keyboards.
